title: quercus djiringensis
intro: Quercus djiringensis, also known as the Tibetan white oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of Tibet. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, with potential medicinal properties including lowering blood pressure and reducing cholesterol levels. The health benefits of Quercus djiringensis are said to include improving cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of certain cancers, and boosting the immune system. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of the tree are used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
